steps.title: Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis
steps.text: What is the recommended initial pharmacotherapy for Oligoarticular or Polyarticular JIA at the primary/secondary level?
steps.rationale: NSAIDs (e.g., ibuprofen 10 mg/kg/dose every 6–8 hours) and intraarticular steroids are first-line options if there's low disease activity and no joint contractures.

steps.title: Juvenile Idiopathic Arthritis
steps.text: What is the recommended action for patients presenting with systemic JIA, psoriatic JIA, enthesitis-related arthritis, or non-specific JIA?
steps.rationale: All cases of systemic, psoriatic, enthesitis-related, and non-specific JIA should be referred to tertiary care for specialist management.
